description: Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a medium and small campus deployment with Underlay RP placement at any VTEP or Aggregate with PIM-BIDIR, traffic loss will be observed due to a bud node scenario.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl?

Feature Caveat: VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl - Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a medium and small campus deployment with Underlay RP placement at any VTEP or Aggregate with PIM-BIDIR, traffic loss will be observed due to a bud node scenario.

description: Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a 3-clos data center design when there is link failure between the VTEP and SPINE, the Underlay Multicast routing path towards the RP picks up a Leaf VTEP as a Transit Router, which can cause traffic loss.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what limitation is mentioned for VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl?

Feature Caveat: VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl - Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a 3-clos data center design when there is link failure between the VTEP and SPINE, the Underlay Multicast routing path towards the RP picks up a Leaf VTEP as a Transit Router, which can cause traffic loss.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: PIM RPF override with nexthop IP address is not working as expected. Scenario: When RPF override is configured, the route for the nexthop must be resolved. After a Route Resolver (RR) succeeds, the system was supposed to check the routes for the nexthop IP. However, route entries were not being updated correctly.
symptom: PIM RPF override with nexthop IP address is not working as expected.
scenario: When RPF override is configured, the route for the nexthop must be resolved. After a Route Resolver (RR) succeeds, the system was supposed to check the routes for the nexthop IP. However, route entries were not being updated correctly.

description: Symptom: BGP neighbor session is stuck in the connect state. Scenario: The BGP neighbor password was changed for the respective switches using a non-CLI method. Subsequently, a switch reboot was performed on only one side of the switch pair. Workaround: On the non-upgraded platform, it is necessary to run the clear bgp command.
symptom: BGP neighbor session is stuck in the connect state.
scenario: The BGP neighbor password was changed for the respective switches using a non-CLI method. Subsequently, a switch reboot was performed on only one side of the switch pair.
workaround: On the non-upgraded platform, it is necessary to run the clear bgp command.

description: Symptom: Client ARP entries are not aging out on the VSX peer switch. Scenario: When an ARP entry is learned and synced to the VSX peer, followed by a client IP change at the end host, the old ARP entries do not age out on the switch. Workaround: Manually clear the ARP entries on the respective VSX peer switch.
symptom: Client ARP entries are not aging out on the VSX peer switch.

workaround: Manually clear the ARP entries on the respective VSX peer switch.

description: Symptom: Interface remains down post a CoA port bounce, with interface details reason showing as Authorization Change. Scenario: On doing a admin shut on the port during the Port-Bounce duration, the interface continues to remain in the down state even after the port-bounce duration. Workaround: Reset the configuration to default by using the default interface <IFNAME> command. This brings the interface back up.

workaround: Reset the configuration to default by using the default interface <IFNAME> command. This brings the interface back up.

description: Symptom: When trying to snmpset VlanStaticName object, customer will see the vlan name briefly change and then change back to the old name. The name is also never updated in DB or CLI. Scenario: When trying to snmpset dot1q/ieee VlanStaticName. Workaround: Change vlan name via CLI instead of through snmpset.
symptom: When trying to snmpset VlanStaticName object, customer will see the vlan name briefly change and then change back to the old name. The name is also never updated in DB or CLI.

description: Symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed. This happened after inserting 1G transceiver into 50G interface mode on CX8360. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports. this will enable only even number ports for 50G use, the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues observed while inserting 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in Physical Port Bug 329507?

Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): Symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed when inserting a 1G transceiver into a 50G interface mode on CX8360. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, grouping in 4 X 25G ports into 2 X 50G ports enables only even-numbered ports for 50G use, while the remaining two odd-numbered ports should be empty. Network issues occur when inserting a 1G transceiver into an empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid combining 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

description: Symptom: Kernel panic occurred while collecting support files at switch. Scenario: When multicast traffic passing through the switch. The logs piled up over a period of time, which can result in an increased system memory consumption. Workaround: Disable or enable PIM, or restart PIM daemon.

description: Symptom: Not possible to set dot1qVlanStaticEgressPorts and dot1qVlanStaticUntaggedPorts MIB objects using a HEX value, only string was supported. Scenario: When trying to snmpset or read values from snmp walk, these two objects will now appear in Hex-String form. Workaround: Pre-fix, string can still be used to successfully set and read the object values. Post-fix, clients will need to use HEX.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.
scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.
workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: PIM RPF override with nexthop IP address is not working as expected. Scenario: When RPF override is configured, the route for the nexthop must be resolved. After a Route Resolver (RR) succeeds, the system was supposed to check the routes for the nexthop IP. However, route entries were not being updated correctly.
symptom: PIM RPF override with nexthop IP address is not working as expected.
scenario: When RPF override is configured, the route for the nexthop must be resolved. After a Route Resolver (RR) succeeds, the system was supposed to check the routes for the nexthop IP. However, route entries were not being updated correctly.

messages.content: Under what scenario does Bug 338339 occur?

PIM-SM (Bug ID 338339): When RPF override is configured, the route for the nexthop must be resolved. After a Route Resolver (RR) succeeds, the system was supposed to check the routes for the nexthop IP. However, route entries were not being updated correctly.

description: Symptom: When trying to snmpset VlanStaticName object, customer will see the vlan name briefly change and then change back to the old name. The name is also never updated in DB or CLI. Scenario: When trying to snmpset dot1q/ieee VlanStaticName. Workaround: Change vlan name via CLI instead of through snmpset.

description: Symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed. This happened after inserting 1G transceiver into 50G interface mode on CX8360. Scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports. this will enable only even number ports for 50G use, the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues observed while inserting 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port. Workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.
symptom: The 50G interface is not coming up and network issues are observed. This happened after inserting 1G transceiver into 50G interface mode on CX8360.
scenario: Using system interface-group X speed 50g, group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports. this will enable only even number ports for 50G use, the remaining two odd number ports should be empty. Network issues observed while inserting 1G transceiver into a empty disabled port.
workaround: Avoid a combination of 1G and 50G interfaces within the same interface-group.

messages.content: Under what scenario does Bug 329507 occur?

Physical Port (Bug ID 329507): Using system interface-group X speed 50g, group in 4 X 25G ports into to 2 X 50G ports. This will enable only even-numbered ports for 50G use, while the remaining two odd-numbered ports should be empty. Network issues observed when inserting a 1G transceiver into an empty disabled port.

description: Symptom: Kernel panic occurred while collecting support files at switch. Scenario: When multicast traffic passing through the switch. The logs piled up over a period of time, which can result in an increased system memory consumption. Workaround: Disable or enable PIM, or restart PIM daemon.

description: Symptom: Not possible to set dot1qVlanStaticEgressPorts and dot1qVlanStaticUntaggedPorts MIB objects using a HEX value, only string was supported. Scenario: When trying to snmpset or read values from snmp walk, these two objects will now appear in Hex-String form. Workaround: Pre-fix, string can still be used to successfully set and read the object values. Post-fix, clients will need to use HEX.

VLAN (Bug ID 302131): When attempting to use SNMP set or read values from an SNMP walk, these two objects will now appear in Hex-String form.
